sections.paragraph:
ἤ particle involving options

—1. marker of an alternative (disjunctive particle) or, between single words Mt 5:17; Mk 3:4; Lk 2:24; J 2:6; Ro 1:21; Gal 3:15. In a declarative sequence ἤ... ἤ either... or Mt 6:24; Lk 16:13; 1 Cor 14:6. Also interrogatively (direct or indirect) Mk 13:35 (ἤ... ἤ... ἤ... ἤ whether... or... or... or); Mt 26:53; Ro 3:29; w. an interrogative word (mostly τίς or τί) Mt 7:9; Lk 14:31; J 9:21; Ro 3:1; 1 Cor 7:16.

—2. marker indicating comparison (with or without preceding comparative)than, rather than Mt 10:15; Mk 10:25; Lk 9:13; Ac 4:19; 17:21; 1 Cor 9:15; numerical contrast introduced by θέλω 14:19. —πρὶν ἤ before Mt 1:18; Mk 14:30; Lk 2:26; Ac 25:16.
